channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lenovo just launched the lightest AMD Ryzen AI laptop ever; ThinkPad X13 Gen 6 tops scale at just over 2 lbs
item.description: Lenovo has unveiled the ThinkPad X13 Gen 6 which is super-light at just 2 lbs and comes with the latest AMD or Intel AI CPUs.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Lenovo has put an AI chip into a monitor that not only controls it, but can also give AI powers to your PC
item.description: Lenovo's AI Display is a screen with a discrete NPU inside that can transfer AI powers to your non-AI PC.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Everything you need to know about the Oscars’ AI controversy
item.description: Sometimes, authenticity can be a film’s most special effect. It took months for Best Actress front-runner Mikey Madison to learn how to pole dance like the titular exotic dancer in Anora and for her fellow nominee Timothée Chalamet to passably play guitar as Bob Dylan in A Complete Unknown.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve, to boost the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Leaked iPhone 17 schematics show the Apple phones could be getting a speaker redesign
item.description: It seems the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max could be in line for a modified speaker configuration.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Private lunar lander Blue Ghost aces moon touchdown with a special delivery for NASA
item.description: A private lunar lander has touched down on the moon, delivering a drill and other experiments for NASA.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Who at DOGE has access to U.S. intelligence secrets? Democrats are demanding answers
item.description: Democratic lawmakers demanded answers from billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency on Thursday as concerns about who has access to America’s most sensitive information continue to dog Trump administration efforts to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rust 1.85 arrives with long-awaited async closures
item.description: Rust 1.85, the latest version of the burgeoning language positioned for speed, safety, and ease of use, has arrived, with new capabilities including async closures. The release also constitutes a stabilization of the Rust 2024 edition of the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This is probably the best Windows alternative to the M4 Mac Mini: AMD-powered mini PC can drive four 8K monitors and has two 2.5Gb Ethernet connectors
item.description: The AuBox 8745 mini PC from Chuwi could rival the Apple M4 Mac Mini - but how does it shape up in terms of performance?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hollywood’s obsession with AI-enabled ‘perfection’ is making movies less human
item.description: The notion of authenticity in the movies has moved a step beyond the merely realistic. More and more, expensive and time-consuming fixes to minor issues of screen realism have become the work of statistical data renderings—the visual or aural products of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: A.S. Hamrah, Fast Company

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon claims to cut quantum computing timeline by 5 years with new Ocelot chip
item.description: Amazon Web Services has unveiled a new quantum computing chip, Ocelot, that the company claims could reduce error correction costs by up to 90% compared to traditional approaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government’s reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Overcoming data compliance and security challenges in the age of AI
item.description: We are in the era of artificial intelligence (AI), and businesses are unlocking unprecedented opportunities for growth and efficiency.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Just what is an ‘IT worker’ now? The definition is changing
item.description: Not so long ago, the definition of an IT worker was pretty simple: they were largely confined to central roles related to hardware, network infrastructure, and software development in centralized IT departments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Five takeaways from Cisco Live EMEA
item.description: Cisco held its European user event in Amsterdam this month, presenting a good mix of industry trends, product innovation, and vision from Cisco as to where they see the market going.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18 expands cloud-native networking
item.description: Red Hat is updating its OpenShift platform with a series of capabilities that will provide more advanced networking and virtualization functionality for cloud-native deployments. OpenShift is Red Hat’s commercially supported Kubernetes distribution.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s Vision Pro enters Industry 4.0 with Dassault Systèmes
item.description: Are you ready for Vision Pro week? Well, you should be as hot on the heels of some highly significant enterprise-focused visionOS improvements, Apple has entered a new initiative that puts its device at the heart of future manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
